revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965
== See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965
== See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965 == See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965 == See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965 == See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965 == See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965 == See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft|12=draft}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=draft|12=draft}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft|12=draft}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=draft|12=draft}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft|12=draft}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|ja|帝紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=draft|12=draft}} and Prince {{ill|Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason (kabane)|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965
== See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hieda no Are|ja|稗田阿礼|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ō no Yasumaro|ja|太安万侶|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Prince Toneri|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 上毛野三千 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[天武天皇]]10年（[[681年]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大錦下]] | 主君 = [[天武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野]][[キミ (カバネ)|君]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''上毛野 三千'''（かみつけの の みちぢ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[大錦下]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣氏]]については、『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別・右京皇別に[[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣氏]]と同祖とあり、『[[日本書紀]]』の記述からも[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫とされている<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>。
『書紀』巻第二十九によると、天武天皇10年（681年）3月に天皇は[[大極殿]]（おおあんどの）から詔して、『[[帝紀]]』（すめらみことのふみ、天皇系譜の記録）と上古（いにしえ）の諸事（もろもろのこと）の記定作業を命じている。この撰定者の中で、三千の名前は[[川島皇子|川嶋皇子]]・[[忍壁皇子]]らに次ぐ七番目に記載されており、皇族以外の人臣としては一番目に当たる。この時の位階は[[大錦下]]（たいきんげ）であった<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇10年3月17日条</ref>。
しかし、この事業は完成せず、同年8月、三千はこの世を去った<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇10年8月11日条</ref>。
天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）11月の[[八色の姓]]で、上毛野君氏は一族の[[上毛野稚子|稚子]]の奮戦や三千の労など、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を授与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『コンサイス日本人名辞典 改訂新版』p359（[[三省堂]]、1993年） * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[中臣大島]] *[[撰善言司]] *[[稗田阿礼]] *[[太安万侶]] *[[古事記]] *[[舎人親王]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の みちち}} [[Category:上毛野氏|みちち]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:681年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[上毛野三千]] to [[Kamitsukeno no Michichi]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 上毛野三千 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[天武天皇]]10年（[[681年]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大錦下]] | 主君 = [[天武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野]][[キミ (カバネ)|君]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''上毛野 三千'''（かみつけの の みちぢ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[大錦下]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣氏]]については、『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別・右京皇別に[[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣氏]]と同祖とあり、『[[日本書紀]]』の記述からも[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫とされている<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>。
『書紀』巻第二十九によると、天武天皇10年（681年）3月に天皇は[[大極殿]]（おおあんどの）から詔して、『[[帝紀]]』（すめらみことのふみ、天皇系譜の記録）と上古（いにしえ）の諸事（もろもろのこと）の記定作業を命じている。この撰定者の中で、三千の名前は[[川島皇子|川嶋皇子]]・[[忍壁皇子]]らに次ぐ七番目に記載されており、皇族以外の人臣としては一番目に当たる。この時の位階は[[大錦下]]（たいきんげ）であった<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇10年3月17日条</ref>。
しかし、この事業は完成せず、同年8月、三千はこの世を去った<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇10年8月11日条</ref>。
天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）11月の[[八色の姓]]で、上毛野君氏は一族の[[上毛野稚子|稚子]]の奮戦や三千の労など、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を授与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『コンサイス日本人名辞典 改訂新版』p359（[[三省堂]]、1993年） * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[中臣大島]] *[[撰善言司]] *[[稗田阿礼]] *[[太安万侶]] *[[古事記]] *[[舎人親王]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi (kabane)]] | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] during the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|qq=}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|qq=}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965
== See Also == * {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ōshima|qq=}} * {{ill|Senzengonotsukasa|ja|撰善言司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senzengonotsukasa|qq=}} * [[:en:Hieda no Are|Hieda no Are]] * [[:en:Ō no Yasumaro|Ō no Yasumaro]] * [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]
{{translated page|ja|上毛野三千|version=70346678|comment=Imported full ja history}}

According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|qq=}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[:en:681|681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi (kabane)]] | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|qq=}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|qq=}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|qq=}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|qq=}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|qq=|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|qq=|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野三千]] {{Infobox person|death_date=0681|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|qid=Q55406948}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55406951]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Michiji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55406951]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Michiji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[:en:681|681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi (kabane)]] | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|qq=|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|qq=|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965

This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野三千]] {{Infobox person|death_date=0681|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|WD=Q55406948}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406951]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Michiji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406951]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Michiji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ([[:en:681|681]]) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi (kabane)]] | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965
