'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the [[:en:Teiki|Teiki]] (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Osakabe]], and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of [[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Sujin}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason (kabane)}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Michichi (上毛野 三千) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[Tenmu era]], 10th year ({{ill|681|en|681|WD=Q30342|lt=681}}) | other_names = | honorific_prefix = | honorific_suffix = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōkinoshita]] (大錦下) | lord = [[Emperor Tenmu]] | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi (kabane)}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Sujin}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Osakabe}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason (kabane)}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}}
== References == * ''Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary,'' Revised Edition, p. 359 (Sanseido, 1993) * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume V, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988 * ''Formation of the Ancient Japanese State,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965

Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Osakabe}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason (kabane)}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Michichi''' (上毛野 三千) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
== Biography == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, to which Michichi belonged, is noted in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under the Left and Right Imperial Descendants sections, identifying them as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'s eldest son, {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Sujin, 48th Year, 1st Month, 10th Day.</ref>
According to Volume 29 of the ''Nihon Shoki,'' in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681), a decree was issued from the {{ill|Ōandom|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōandom|1=Daigokuden|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} (Great Hall) ordering the compilation of the {{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki|1=Teiki}} (Imperial Genealogies) and records of ancient matters. Michichi's name appears seventh among those assigned to this task, following Prince {{ill|Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima|1=Prince Kawashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and Prince {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}}, and he was the first commoner listed.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 3rd Month, 17th Day.</ref> At that time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Ōkinoshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}.
This endeavor, however, was never completed. Later that same year, in the 8th month, Michichi passed away.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 10th Year, 8th Month, 11th Day.</ref>
In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684), the {{ill|Yatsurano Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurano Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform conferred the kabane title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason (kabane)|1=Ason}} on the Kamitsukeno clan, acknowledging the contributions of Michichi and others in their service to the court.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 11th Month, 1st Day.</ref>
